ORDER GRANTING IN PART, AND DENYING IN PART PLAINTIFF CAROL A. ENGELBRECHT'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDERS RE. DEPOSITIONS OF PLAINTIFF AND HER ATTORNEY THE COURT CONSIDERED THE PAPERS FILED BY THE PARTIES AND GRANTED THE MOTION IN PART AND DENIED THE MOTION IN PART. AS TO THE DEPOSITON OF JOHN BABA, THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS HE IS A PERCIPIENT WITNESS AND DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO OBTAIN HIS TESTIMONY AS PERCIPIENT WITNESS. MR. BABA IS ENTITLED TO ASSERT PRIVILEGE AS TO ATTORNEY-CLIENT COMMUNICATIONS AND WORK PRODUCT, BUT AS HE INTERACTED WITH CONTRACTORS HE WAS ACTING PREDOMINANTLY IN A BUSINESS CAPACITY. AS TO DEPOSTION OF CAROL A.
